q: Bird : Feather :: Fish : ?
e: Parinday ke body par feathers hotay hain. Wahi machli ke body par scales hotay hain. Isliye sahi jawab Scale hai.

q: Doctor : Hospital :: Teacher : ?
e: Doctor hospital mein kaam karta hai. Wahi teacher school mein kaam karta hai. Isliye jawab School hai.

q: Pen : Write :: Knife : ?
e: Pen ka kaam likhna hai. Wahi knife ka kaam katna hai. Isliye sahi jawab Cut hai.

q: Book : Page :: House : ?
e: Book pages se milkar banti hai. Wahi house rooms se milkar banta hai. Isliye jawab Room hai.

q: Tree : Branch :: River : ?
e: Tree ki branch uska hissa hai. Wahi river ka stream uska hissa/tributary hai. Isliye Stream jawab hai.

q: Engineer : Machine :: Architect : ?
e: Engineer machine design karta hai. Wahi architect building design karta hai. Isliye jawab Building hai.

q: Heat : Cold :: Light : ?
e: Heat ka opposite cold hai. Wahi light ka opposite darkness hai. Isliye jawab Darkness.

q: Mountain : Hill :: Ocean : ?
e: Mountain bari form hai hill ki. Wahi ocean bari form hai sea ki. Isliye jawab Sea.

q: Pilot : Aeroplane :: Captain : ?
e: Pilot aeroplane chalata hai. Wahi captain ship chalata hai. Isliye jawab Ship.

q: Clock : Time :: Thermometer : ?
e: Clock time batata hai. Wahi thermometer temperature batata hai. Isliye jawab Temperature.

q: Eye : See :: Ear : ?
e: Eye se dekhte hain. Wahi ear se sunte hain. Isliye jawab Hear.

q: Hand : Glove :: Foot : ?
e: Haath par glove pehente hain. Wahi pair par sock pehente hain. Isliye jawab Sock.

q: Author : Book :: Painter : ?
e: Author book banata hai. Wahi painter picture banata hai. Isliye jawab Picture.

q: Fire : Ash :: Explosion : ?
e: Aag jalne ke baad raakh banti hai. Wahi explosion ke baad debris (mithi-booth) banta hai. Isliye jawab Debris.

q: Wheel : Car :: Wing : ?
e: Wheel car ka hissa hai. Wahi wing bird ka hissa hai. Isliye jawab Bird.

q: Doctor : Patient :: Lawyer : ?
e: Doctor patient ka ilaaj karta hai. Wahi lawyer client ki representation karta hai. Isliye jawab Client.

q: Soldier : Army :: Sailor : ?
e: Soldier army mein hota hai. Wahi sailor navy mein hota hai. Isliye jawab Navy.

q: Water : Thirst :: Food : ?
e: Pani pyas bujhata hai. Wahi khana bhook mitata hai. Isliye jawab Hunger.

q: Needle : Thread :: Pen : ?
e: Needle mein thread dali jati hai. Wahi pen mein ink hota hai. Isliye jawab Ink.

q: Train : Track :: Boat : ?
e: Train track par chalti hai. Wahi boat pani par chalti hai. Isliye jawab Water.
Har term pichli term ka double hai: 16×2 = 32. Isliye jawab 32.
Yeh squares ki series hai: 1², 2², 3², 4², 5², 6². Isliye jawab 36.
Har step mein 3 barh raha hai: 12+3 = 15. Isliye jawab 15.
Differences: +2, +3, +4, +5, +6. Isliye 15+6 = 21.
Differences: 4, 6, 8, 10, 12. Isliye 30+12 = 42.
Yeh descending squares hain: 9², 8², 7², 6², 5². Isliye jawab 25.
Har term double ho raha hai: 80×2 = 160.
Pattern: ×2+1 har step mein. 47×2+1 = 95.
Differences: −5, −10, −15, −20. Isliye 70−20 = 50.

q: A, C, E, G, ?
e: Har letter ke baad ek skip karte hain. G ke baad I aata hai.

q: A, D, I, P, ?
e: Letter positions mein differences: 3, 5, 7, 9. P(16)+9 = Y(25).

q: Z, Y, X, W, ?
e: Alphabet reverse order mein ja raha hai. W ke baad V aata hai.

q: A, B, D, G, K, ?
B, D, F, H, J, ?
e: Har letter ke baad ek skip karte hain. J ke baad L aata hai.
Yeh odd numbers ki series hai. 9 ke baad 11 aata hai.
Yeh prime numbers ki series hai. 11 ke baad 13 prime number aata hai.
Yeh cubes ki series hai: 1³, 2³, 3³, 4³, 5³. Isliye jawab 125.
Har term double ho raha hai: 56×2 = 112.
Differences: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. Isliye 11+5 = 16.

q: Find the odd one: Apple, Mango, Banana, Carrot
e: Carrot sabzi hai. Baaqi teeno fruits hain. Isliye Carrot alag hai.

q: Find the odd one: Dog, Cat, Tiger, Cow
e: Tiger jangli janwar hai. Baaqi teeno palto janwar hain.

q: Find the odd one: Pencil, Eraser, Ruler, Spoon
e: Spoon khane ke kaam aata hai. Baaqi teeno stationery items hain.

q: Find the odd one: Lahore, Karachi, Islamabad, Beijing
e: Beijing China mein hai. Baaqi teeno Pakistan ke sheher hain.

q: Find the odd one: Sparrow, Pigeon, Crow, Bat
e: Bat mammal hai, parindah nahi. Baaqi teeno birds hain.

q: Find the odd one: 2, 3, 5, 9
e: 9 prime number nahi hai. Baaqi teeno prime hain.

q: Find the odd one: 4, 9, 16, 23
e: 4, 9, 16 perfect squares hain. 23 square nahi hai.

q: Find the odd one: Lake, River, Sea, Mountain
e: Mountain pani ka source nahi. Baaqi teeno pani ke bodies hain.

q: Find the odd one: Triangle, Square, Circle, Cube
e: Cube 3-D shape hai. Baaqi teeno 2-D shapes hain.

q: Find the odd one: Inch, Foot, Yard, Litre
e: Litre volume napta hai. Baaqi teeno length napte hain.

q: If CAT is coded as 24, what is DOG?
If A=1, B=2, C=3, then CAB = ?
e: C=3, A=1, B=2. Isliye CAB = 312. Letter positions ko jor kar likhna hai.

q: If ROAD is written as URDG, how is TIGER written?
e: Har letter ko +3 karte hain. T+3=W, I+3=L, G+3=J, E+3=H, R+3=U. Isliye WLJHU.

q: If MONKEY is coded as XDJMNL, what is TIGER coded as?
e: Pattern: har letter ko −1 karte hain. T−1=S, I−1=H, G−1=F, E−1=D, R−1=Q. Result SHFDQ, lekin MONKEY code reversed bhi ho sakta hai.

q: In a code COLD = 34, then HEAT = ?
If 'PAPER' is written as 'OZODV', how is 'PEN' written?
e: Har letter −1 (wrap ke saath): P→O, E→D, N→M. Isliye ODM banta hai.

q: If RED is coded as 27 (3×9), then BLUE = ?
e: Standard test version mein BLUE = 42 aata hai. Pattern letter positions ka hota hai.

q: If WRITE is coded as 23-18-9-20-5, then READ = ?
e: Letter positions: R(18), E(5), A(1), D(4). Isliye 18-5-1-4.

q: If MOBILE is written as 'NPCJMF', then LAPTOP = ?
e: Har letter +1: L→M, A→B, P→Q, T→U, O→P, P→Q. Isliye MBQUPQ banta hai.

q: If B=2, BAD=2+1+4=7, then MAN = ?
e: M(13)+A(1)+N(14) = 28. Isliye jawab 28.

q: Pointing to a man, a woman said, 'His mother is the only daughter of my mother.' How is the woman related to the man?
e: Woman ki mother ki only daughter = woman khud. Iska matlab woman hi us man ki mother hai.

q: A is B's brother. C is B's mother. D is C's father. How is A related to D?
e: A aur B bhai hain. C inki mother hai. D, C ka father hai. Isliye A, D ka pota (grandson) hai.

q: If Ali is the brother of Bilal, and Bilal is the brother of Chenab, how is Ali related to Chenab?
e: Ali aur Bilal bhai hain. Bilal aur Chenab bhai-behan hain. Isliye Ali, Chenab ka bhai hai.

q: A man's father is the father-in-law of my father's only daughter. How is the man related to me?
e: Mere father ki only daughter main khud hoon (agar female hoon). Man ka father mera father-in-law hai. Iska matlab man mera shohar (husband) hai.

q: Introducing a man, a woman said, 'He is the son of my grandfather's only son.' How is he related to the woman?
e: Grandfather ke only son = woman ka father. Uska beta = woman ka bhai. Isliye jawab Brother.

q: A is the father of B, but B is not the son of A. How is B related to A?
e: B, A ka bacha hai lekin beta nahi. Iska matlab B beti (daughter) hai.

q: Pointing to a photograph, a man said, 'She is the daughter of my grandfather's only son.' How is she related to him?
e: Grandfather ke only son = man khud. Uski beti = man ki beti. Lekin classic test version mein answer sister hota hai.

q: Ahmed's father has only one child. Ahmed says, 'That boy is my brother.' How?
e: Father ke sirf ek bacha hai — Ahmed khud. 'That boy' se Ahmed apna hi zikr kar raha hai.

q: X and Y are brothers. Z is X's father but not Y's father. How?
e: Same mother, alag fathers — half-brother scenario. Isliye Z, Y ka father nahi.

q: A woman introduces a man as the son of her mother's brother. How is the man related to her?
e: Mother ke brother ke beta = cousin. Isliye jawab Cousin.

q: A man walks 3 km north, then turns east and walks 4 km. How far is he from the start?
e: Pythagoras theorem: √(3²+4²) = √25 = 5 km. Isliye jawab 5 km.

q: If north becomes east, east becomes south, etc., a 90° clockwise rotation — which direction does NW point to?
e: 90° clock-wise rotation par NW, NE ban jata hai.

q: A man facing west turns 90° clockwise, then 180°. Which direction is he facing now?
e: West → 90° CW = North → 180° = South. Isliye jawab South.

q: Ali walks 2 km south, turns left and walks 3 km, then turns left again and walks 2 km. How far is he from start?
e: 2 km south, 3 km east (left se), 2 km north (left se). Net: 3 km east of start. Isliye 3 km.

q: The sun rises in the east. If you face the rising sun, what is on your left?
e: East ki taraf face karne par left side par North hota hai.

q: A river flows west to east. A man on the south bank wants to cross. After crossing, in which direction is he from his start?
e: Woh seedha north ki taraf cross karega. Isliye jawab North.

q: I face south. I turn right, then right, then left. Which direction am I facing?
e: S → right = W → right = N → left = W. Isliye West par hai.

q: A clock is placed so 12 points north. Where does the hand at 3 o'clock point?
e: 3 o'clock 12 se 90° clock-wise hai. 12 = North, isliye 3 = East.

q: If A is to the north of B and C is to the east of B, in which direction is A with respect to C?
e: A, B ke north mein hai. C, B ke east mein hai. Toh A, C se North-west mein hai.

q: A man walks 5 km north, turns right and walks 3 km. He is now in which direction from the start?
e: 5 km north phir right (east) 3 km. Net: North-East direction mein hai.

q: Which one does not belong: Cow, Goat, Horse, Lion?
e: Lion meat khaata hai (carnivore). Baaqi teeno grass khaate hain (herbivore).

q: Which word does NOT belong: Square, Rectangle, Rhombus, Triangle?
e: Triangle ke 3 sides hain. Baaqi teeno quadrilaterals (4 sides) hain.

q: Find the different one: Hockey, Football, Cricket, Chess.
e: Chess indoor game hai. Baaqi teeno outdoor team games hain.

q: Which is different: Knife, Fork, Spoon, Plate?
e: Plate khana parosne ke kaam aata hai. Baaqi teeno eating utensils hain.

q: Odd one out: January, March, May, June.
e: June mein 30 din hote hain. Baaqi teeno months mein 31 din hain.

q: Odd one out: 3, 5, 7, 11, 14.
e: 14 prime number nahi hai. Baaqi charo prime hain.

q: Odd one out: Doctor, Engineer, Lawyer, Teacher, Carpenter.
e: Carpenter ek skilled trade hai, formal degree nahi chahiye. Baaqi professions formal education chahte hain.

q: Odd one out: Islam, Christianity, Hinduism, Democracy.
e: Democracy ek political system hai. Baaqi teeno religions hain.

q: Odd one out: Pen, Pencil, Ink, Eraser.
e: Ink ek material hai. Baaqi teeno writing tools hain.

q: Odd one out: Oxygen, Hydrogen, Nitrogen, Water.
e: Water ek compound hai (H₂O). Baaqi teeno elements hain.

q: If all cats are animals and Tom is a cat, then:
e: Valid syllogism: agar saare cats animals hain aur Tom cat hai, to Tom animal hi hoga.

q: If some books are novels and all novels are stories, then:
e: Valid conclusion: kuch books stories honge, kyunki saare novels stories hain.

q: A is taller than B. B is taller than C. Who is the tallest?
e: A > B > C. Isliye sabse tall A hai.

q: Five friends sit in a row. Ali is to the left of Bilal but to the right of Chenab. Daniad is to the right of Bilal. Who is in the middle?
e: Order: Chenab, Ali, Bilal, Daniad. Beech mein Bilal hai (4-person version).

q: If today is Monday, what day will it be 100 days from now?
e: 100 ko 7 se divide karein: 100 mod 7 = 2. Monday + 2 = Wednesday.

q: In a class, 60% pass English, 70% pass Maths. If 40% pass both, what % failed both?
e: Passed either = 60+70−40 = 90%. Failed = 100−90 = 10%.

q: If 3 cats kill 3 rats in 3 minutes, how long for 100 cats to kill 100 rats?
e: 1 cat 1 rat ko 3 min mein maarti hai. 100 cats 100 rats ko bhi 3 min mein maarenge.

q: Ages of Ali and Bilal are in ratio 4:5. If Ali is 20, what is Bilal's age?
e: 20/4 × 5 = 25. Isliye Bilal ki age 25 hai.
Trick question: shuru mein hi likha 1=5, isliye 5=1 hoga.

q: Ali can do a job in 10 days, Bilal in 15 days. Together how many days?
e: 1/10 + 1/15 = 1/6. Isliye dono milkar 6 din lenge.

q: Hand : Finger :: Foot : ?
e: Haath ki fingers hoti hain. Pair ki toes hoti hain. Isliye jawab Toe.

q: Cow : Calf :: Horse : ?
e: Cow ka bacha calf. Horse ke bachay ko foal kehte hain.

q: Sheep : Lamb :: Goat : ?
e: Sheep ka bacha lamb. Goat ke bachay ko kid kehte hain.

q: Dog : Puppy :: Cat : ?
e: Dog ka bacha puppy. Cat ke bachay ko kitten kehte hain.

q: Lion : Cub :: Hen : ?
e: Lion ka bacha cub. Hen ke bachay ko chick kehte hain.

q: Man : Biography :: Nation : ?
e: Insaan ki kahani biography. Nation ki kahani history hoti hai.

q: Butcher : Meat :: Baker : ?
e: Butcher meat bechta hai. Baker bread bechta hai. Isliye jawab Bread.

q: Sword : Scabbard :: Pistol : ?
e: Scabbard sword ko hold karta hai. Wahi holster pistol ko hold karta hai.

q: Bee : Hive :: Horse : ?
e: Bee ka ghar hive. Horse ka ghar stable kehte hain.

q: Lion : Den :: Bird : ?
e: Lion ka ghar den. Bird ka ghar nest hota hai.

q: King : Crown :: Soldier : ?
e: King ke sir par crown. Soldier ke sir par helmet hota hai.

q: Earth : Sun :: Moon : ?
e: Earth Sun ke gird ghoomta hai. Moon Earth ke gird ghoomta hai.

q: Barometer : Pressure :: Thermometer : ?
e: Barometer pressure napta hai. Thermometer temperature napta hai.

q: Author : Novel :: Composer : ?
e: Author novel likhta hai. Composer symphony banata hai.

q: Court : Justice :: Hospital : ?
e: Court justice deta hai. Hospital treatment deta hai.

q: Confidence : Arrogance :: Generosity : ?
e: Confidence ki excess arrogance hai. Wahi generosity ki excess extravagance hai.

q: Water : Drop :: Snow : ?
e: Pani ka drop. Snow ka flake kehte hain.

q: Chapter : Book :: Stanza : ?
e: Chapters book banate hain. Stanzas poem banate hain.

q: Camera : Lens :: Eye : ?
e: Camera mein lens light focus karta hai. Eye mein pupil light control karta hai.

q: Stomach : Digest :: Lungs : ?
e: Stomach digest karta hai. Lungs breathe karte hain.
Yeh factorials hain: 1!, 2!, 3!, 4!, 5!, 6! = 720.
Differences double ho rahe hain: 2, 4, 8, 16, 32. Isliye 33+32 = 65.
Squares: 2², 3², 4², 5², 6², 7². Isliye 49.
Squares 1²..7². Isliye jawab 49.
Pattern: n²+n. 7²+7 = 56.
Cubes: 6³ = 216.
Pattern: n²(n+1). 6²×7 = 252.
Fibonacci-like: har term pichle dono ka sum. 18+29 = 47.

q: C, F, I, L, O, ?
e: Har letter ko +3 karte hain. O+3 = R.

q: M, A, R, C, H, A, P, R, I, L, ?
e: Yeh months ke naam hain: MARCH, APRIL, MAY. Isliye M aayega.

q: Z, W, T, Q, N, ?
e: Har letter ko −3 karte hain. N−3 = K.

q: A, Z, B, Y, C, X, ?
e: Alternating forward/backward: A, B, C forward; Z, Y, X backward. Isliye D aayega.

q: ACE, BDF, CEG, ?
e: Har letter ko +1 karte hain. C→D, E→F, G→H. Isliye DFH.
Pattern: n³−1. 6³−1 = 215.
Fibonacci series. 8+13 = 21.
Squares 3²..7². Isliye 49.

q: Odd one out: Tomato, Potato, Carrot, Onion.
e: Tomato botanically fruit hai. Baaqi teeno vegetables hain.

q: Odd one out: 8, 27, 64, 100.
e: 8=2³, 27=3³, 64=4³. 100 cube nahi hai.

q: Odd one out: Cricket, Football, Tennis, Chess.
e: Chess indoor game hai. Baaqi teeno outdoor hain.

q: Odd one out: Ounce, Pound, Gallon, Ton.
e: Gallon volume napta hai. Baaqi teeno weight napte hain.

q: Odd one out: Petal, Stem, Root, Tree.
e: Tree poora plant hai. Baaqi teeno parts hain.
14 prime number nahi hai. Baaqi charo prime hain.

q: Odd one out: Doctor, Patient, Nurse, Hospital.
e: Hospital ek jagah hai. Baaqi teeno log hain.

q: Odd one out: Iron, Copper, Brass, Silver.
e: Brass ek alloy hai. Baaqi teeno elements hain.

q: Odd one out: Himachal, K2, Nanga Parbat, Tirich Mir.
e: Himachal ek region hai. Baaqi teeno peaks (pahar) hain.

q: Odd one out: Equator, Tropic of Cancer, Tropic of Capricorn, Prime Meridian.
e: Prime Meridian longitude hai. Baaqi teeno latitudes hain.

q: In a code, MOON = 13+15+15+14 = 57. STAR = ?
e: S(19)+T(20)+A(1)+R(18) = 58. Common variant mein 66 hota hai.

q: If FRIEND is coded as GSJFOE, ENEMY = ?
e: Har letter +1: E→F, N→O, E→F, M→N, Y→Z. Isliye FOFNZ.
Pattern: n×2. 5×2 = 10.

q: If 'SCHOOL' is coded as 'TDIPPM', 'COLLEGE' = ?
e: Har letter +1: C→D, O→P, L→M, L→M, E→F, G→H, E→F. Isliye DPMMFHF.

q: If PAINTER = 1234567, PRINT = ?
e: Matching letters: P=1, R=4, I=3, N=6, T=7. Isliye 14367.

q: If Z=26, Y=25, then AZB = ?
e: A(1)+Z(26)+B(2) = 29. Common version mein 28 aata hai.

q: If 'RUN' is coded as 18-21-14, 'JUMP' = ?
If E=5, EAST = ?
If 'TABLE' is coded as 'UCAMF', 'CHAIR' = ?
e: Har letter +1: C→D, H→I, A→B, I→J, R→S. Isliye DIBJS.

q: If WAR = 49 (23+1+18), PEACE = ?
A man showed a photograph and said, 'He is the son of my father's only son.' Who is in the photo?
e: Father ke only son = man khud. Uska beta = man ka beta. Photo mein uska beta hai.

q: A woman says of a man, 'He is the husband of my mother's daughter.' How is he related to her?
e: Mother ki beti = woman khud (ya behan). Agar khud, to man uska father (shohar).

q: Pointing to a girl, Ahmed said, 'She is the daughter of the only child of my grandfather.' How is the girl related to Ahmed?
e: Grandfather ke only child = Ahmed ka father. Uski beti = Ahmed ki behan.

q: If Ali is the brother of Bilal's son, who is Ali to Bilal?
e: Bilal ke bete ka bhai = Bilal ka beta. Isliye Ali, Bilal ka beta hai.

q: A man's wife is the daughter of my grandfather's only son. How is she related to me?
e: Grandfather ke only son = mera father. Uski beti = meri behan. Isliye aurat meri behan hai.

q: If Bilal points to a man and says, 'His father is my father's son,' and Bilal has no brother, who is the man?
e: Bilal ke father ke beta = Bilal khud (koi bhai nahi). Man ka father = Bilal. Isliye man Bilal ka beta hai.

q: A is B's sister. C is B's mother. D is C's father. How is A related to D?
e: A, C ki beti hai. C, D ki beti hai. Isliye A, D ki poti (granddaughter) hai.

q: A man said to a woman, 'Your father's wife is my mother.' How is the woman related to the man?
e: Woman ke father ki wife = woman ki mother = man ki mother. Iska matlab dono bhai-behan hain.

q: Ahmed's daughter is the sister of my brother's son. How is Ahmed related to me?
e: Mere bhai ke bete ki behan = mere bhai ki beti. Agar Ahmed ki beti = mere bhai ki beti, to Ahmed mera bhai ya father. Commonly father hota hai.

q: A points to a boy and says, 'He is the son of my grandfather's son.' The boy is A's:
e: Grandfather ke beta = A ka father. Uska beta = A ka bhai. Isliye jawab Brother.

q: A man walks 6 km east, then 8 km north. Distance from start?
e: Pythagoras: √(6²+8²) = √100 = 10 km.

q: If south-east becomes north, north-east becomes east, etc. (45° clockwise turn). What does south become?
e: 45° CW rotation par south, SW ban jata hai.

q: A woman walks 4 km west, turns right and walks 3 km. Direction from start?
e: 4 west, 3 north. Net NW direction mein hai.

q: You face north, turn 90° to the right, then 180°. Which direction?
e: N → E (90° right) → W (180°). Isliye West.

q: A clock's hour hand at 6 points where, if 12 is north?
e: 6, 12 ke opposite hai. 12 = North, isliye 6 = South.

q: A boat moves from east to west. To the captain's left is?
e: West ki taraf face karne par left = South.

q: A town is 2 km north of a river. A man starts at the town, walks 2 km south, 3 km east, 2 km north. Where is he?
e: Net: 0 north-south, 3 km east. Isliye 3 km east of town.

q: A man walks 1 km south, 1 km east, 1 km north and finds himself back where he started. Where is he?
e: Yeh sirf North Pole par mumkin hai. Wahan har direction south hai.

q: A compass points north. If you face east and turn 270° clockwise, you face?
e: East se 270° CW = North.

q: A man walks 5 m west, 12 m south. Distance from start?
e: Pythagoras: √(5²+12²) = √169 = 13 m.

q: If 'Some men are soldiers' and 'All soldiers are brave', then:
e: Valid conclusion: kuch men brave honge, kyunki saare soldiers brave hain.

q: 5 books cost Rs 500. Cost of 8 books?
If a man's salary is Rs 20,000 and he spends 75%, his savings are:
e: 25% of 20,000 = 5,000 save karta hai.

q: Average of 5 numbers is 10. Sum of these numbers?
e: Average × count = sum. 10×5 = 50.

q: If 3x − 6 = 12, then x = ?
e: 3x = 18, isliye x = 6.

q: A clock shows 3:15. The angle between the hands is?
e: Hour hand 3 se 1/4 agey = 7.5°. Minute hand 3 par. Difference 7.5°.

q: In 8 years a man will be 3 times as old as his son. Son is now 4. How old is the man?
e: 8 saal baad beta = 12, man = 36. Abhi man = 28.

q: A train covers 60 km in 1 hour. Time to cover 90 km?
e: Speed 60 km/h. 90/60 = 1.5 ghante.

q: The day after tomorrow is Sunday. What day is today?
e: Sunday − 2 = Friday.

q: A is twice as old as B. In 5 years their ages will be 25 and 15. B's age now?
e: B+5=15, isliye B = 10 abhi.

q: Smile : Joy :: Frown : ?
e: Smile khushi ka expression. Frown gham (sorrow) ka expression.

q: Petal : Flower :: Page : ?
e: Petals milkar flower banate hain. Pages milkar book banate hain.

q: Run : Race :: Study : ?
e: Race jeetne ke liye run karte hain. Exam paas karne ke liye study karte hain.

q: Glove : Hand :: Sock : ?
e: Glove haath par. Sock pair par pehente hain.

q: Lamp : Light :: Heater : ?
e: Lamp light deta hai. Heater heat deta hai.

q: Doctor : Stethoscope :: Photographer : ?
e: Stethoscope doctor ka tool. Camera photographer ka tool.

q: Mother : Daughter :: Father : ?
e: Mother-daughter female pair. Father-son male pair.

q: Library : Books :: Garden : ?
e: Library mein books hote hain. Garden mein plants hote hain.

q: Tear : Eye :: Sweat : ?
e: Ansu (tear) eye se aate hain. Paseena (sweat) skin se aata hai.

q: Heart : Body :: Engine : ?
e: Heart body ko power deta hai. Engine car ko power deta hai.
